heroHeadline: China Zero H200 Purchases Despite US Approval; Open-Weight AI Models Overtake US in Global Downloads; Humanoid Robots 90% Chinese
heroSubtitle: Commerce Secretary Lutnick confirmed April 23 that China bought zero Nvidia H200 chips despite January US approval — China is actively choosing Huawei Ascend; China's open-weight AI models surpassed US in global download share for the first time (17.1% vs 15.86%); Chinese humanoid robots captured 90% of global shipments in 2025 with Unitree and AgiBot leading; Tesla delayed FSD rollout in China again as domestic rivals advance
footerNote: Data sourced from MIIT, BIS, Bloomberg, FT, SCMP, CSET, Nikkei Asia, TechInsights, DeepSeek, Huawei Annual Reports, TrendForce, US Trade Representative, CBP, China MOFCOM, Stanford HAI AI Index 2026, House Select Committee on CCP, CNN, NPR, Al Jazeera, TechCrunch, Tom's Hardware, DigiTimes, Semafor, MIT Technology Review, CNBC
